messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Create a small library with one function and a unit test.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "choose-language-and-test-framework",
      "title": "Choose language and test framework",
      "description": "Decide which programming language and unit test framework to use for the small library (e.g., Python + pytest, JavaScript + Jest).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A specific language and test framework are selected and recorded (e.g., Python + pytest)."
    },
    {
      "id": "init-project-structure",
      "title": "Initialize project structure and repo",
      "description": "Create repository layout, initialize version control, and add basic files (README, .gitignore, license) and a simple folder structure for the library and tests.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "choose-language-and-test-framework"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A repository with the agreed folder layout, README, .gitignore, and initial commit exists locally or remotely."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-core-function",
      "title": "Implement the library function",
      "description": "Write the single library function with clear input/output behavior, including inline docstring/comments and minimal error handling.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project-structure"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "The function file exists, is syntactically correct, has a docstring, and example usage is described in comments."
    },
    {
      "id": "write-unit-test",
      "title": "Write unit test for the function",
      "description": "Create one or more unit tests covering expected behavior and at least one edge case using the chosen test framework.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-core-function"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "A test file exists and includes tests that assert the function's expected outputs and an edge case."
    },
    {
      "id": "run-tests-and-fix-issues",
      "title": "Run tests and resolve failures",
      "description": "Execute the test suite, debug any failures, fix the function or tests as needed, and re-run until tests pass reliably.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "write-unit-test"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "All unit tests pass consistently in the test run command without manual intervention."
    },
    {
      "id": "finalize-documentation-and-package",
      "title": "Finalize documentation and prepare package",
      "description": "Update README with usage and install instructions, add version info, and optionally prepare a minimal package file (e.g., setup.cfg or package.json).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "run-tests-and-fix-issues"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README includes usage and test instructions and a basic package metadata file is present for future publishing."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 6,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Sequential top-down decomposition: first decide environment, initialize project, implement the function, then create tests, run/fix them, and finish with documentation/packaging."
}
